endpoints.test: kernel-store arm strictly outside the accuracy-vs-total-system-bytes Pareto hull of {larger, int4, byte-matched text-RAG, distilled}; paired bootstrap α=0.05; full V under F0

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HE5 (verbatim): Measured range: Store 10³–10⁶ records (model-free); accuracy leg 70M–160M. Envelope: Byte claim: store-size axis extrapolates freely; accuracy leg ≤410M without T3; direction to 7B via RETRO's published range. Anchor: RETRO measured 150M–7B with benefit retained (and InstructRetro absorption caveat). Rows HS9–HS12 (verbatim): Inherit constituent experiments; envelope = intersection of the constituent rows (F2/F3/F5/F6).
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §3 HE5 kill criterion, F5 leg (verbatim): 'M4-kernel dead if the kernel-store arm fails to leave the hull of {byte-matched text-RAG, quantized, distilled}; beating only "no store" re-establishes Atlas/RETRO and is reported as not-ours.' HS9 (verbatim): 'Store dies if scaffold-then-discard dominates at all realistic Q; scaffold dies on F6's TOST.' HS11 (verbatim): 'Text-store parity on both ⇒ the kernel's storage contribution is a compression format, not an architecture; KOTK/2 kept as engineering, M4 architectural claim retired.'

corpus_hashes._recipe: sha256 over the sorted concatenation of '<relpath>:<file-sha256>\n' lines for every file under data/<corpus>/
corpus_hashes.store-corpora: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-st (10^3/10^5/10^6 + byte-matched compressed text stores, zstd-JCS discipline)

runner_constraints.note: post-F2 infrastructure decision deferred by the maintainer at GNG-0; these pins are set by ops amendment or at this record's own freeze

title: F5 — content-addressed store accuracy leg: hull test vs byte-matched text-RAG / int4 / distilled / no-store (HE5 full claim; HS9 lifecycle crossover; HS11 structured-store-earns-place; DOUBLE-GATED: f1 PASS + f3 settled + maintainer budget approval)
